steps.title: Calculate Jupiter's weight using the given percentage
steps.content:
Jupiter's weight is 247.5% of Earth's weight:
$$\text{Weight on Jupiter} = 2.475 \times 240.00 = 594.00 \text{ pounds}$$

steps.title: Find x where Saturn weight equals x% of Jupiter weight
steps.content:
Set up the proportion where Saturn's weight is $x$% of Jupiter's weight:
$$250.07 = \frac{x}{100} \times 594.00$$
Solve for $x$:
$$x = \frac{250.07}{594.00} \times 100 \approx 42.10$$
That confirms A is correct.
